skill.name: specdd-do
skill.description: Use when Agent needs to implement a code, docs, tests, task, or spec change under active SpecDD authority.
skill.summary: Guides AI agents to implement changes under active SpecDD authority with proper bootstrap resolution and governance rules.
skill.author: specdd

security_audit.summary: All 11 static analysis findings are false positives. The 9 backtick detections are Markdown inline code formatting for file paths and keywords, not Ruby shell execution. The 2 weak cryptographic algorithm detections correspond to an Apache-2.0 license identifier and the word 'security' in a plain sentence, not actual crypto code. The skill is a SpecDD workflow instruction set with no executable code, network access, or file system manipulation.

low_findings.title: Markdown backticks incorrectly flagged as shell execution
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged Markdown inline code formatting as Ruby or shell backtick execution. All 9 occurrences are legitimate Markdown syntax used to reference file paths, spec names, and keywords. No actual command execution is present.

low_findings.title: License identifier and text word flagged as weak crypto
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged Apache-2.0 license identifier and the word 'security' as weak cryptographic algorithm references. Neither value represents a cryptographic algorithm or function.

content.value_statement: SpecDD projects need clear governance for AI agents making code and task changes. This skill establishes bootstrap resolution, spec chain navigation, and implementation rules so agents work correctly within project authority boundaries.
actual_capabilities: Resolve and apply bootstrap files in the correct order for SpecDD projects
Navigate spec chains to find governing contracts for any target file or task
Identify the nearest local spec that grants write authority before making edits
Enforce implementation rules including authority boundaries and project constraints
Report specs used, files changed, verification results, and remaining uncertainty
Prefer project-local tools and conventions when implementing changes
limitations: Requires an existing SpecDD bootstrap structure to function correctly
Cannot override, loosen, or ignore target project specification contracts
Does not automatically detect when bootstrap files have changed
Limited to projects with explicit write authority defined in governing specs

use_cases.title: Feature Implementation in SpecDD Projects
use_cases.description: A developer working in a SpecDD-governed project needs an AI agent to implement a new feature while respecting all bootstrap rules, spec chains, and authority boundaries.
use_cases.target_user: Software developer in a SpecDD-governed project

use_cases.title: Automated Code Changes Under Governance
use_cases.description: An AI agent making routine code changes needs clear rules about which specs to read, when to verify authority, and how to report results without violating project contracts.
use_cases.target_user: AI agent operating within a development workflow

use_cases.title: Consistent Change Management Across Teams
use_cases.description: A team lead wants all AI-assisted changes to follow the same governance rules, ensuring consistent spec resolution and authority checking across multiple contributors.
use_cases.target_user: Engineering team lead or project manager

prompt_templates.title: Simple Task Under Existing Authority
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to fix a bug in src/parser.js. The project is already a SpecDD project with bootstrap files. Resolve the spec chain, confirm I have write authority, and make the smallest correct change.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: Quick bug fix in a SpecDD project with clear authority

prompt_templates.title: Task With Multiple Spec Files
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to add a new endpoint to the API. Read the bootstrap chain for this project. Then resolve the spec chain for the routes directory. Check both the project-level and local specs for write authority before planning.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: Working with nested spec files and layered authority

prompt_templates.title: Change In Complex Governance Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: We need to refactor the authentication module. Walk the full spec chain from the project root to the auth module directory. Identify all governing constraints. Verify that write authority is granted at the nearest local spec. Do not proceed if any spec contains a Forbids rule for this change.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: Navigating complex governance with constraint verification

prompt_templates.title: Full Workflow With Validation And Reporting
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement the database migration for the new user preferences feature. Follow the SpecDD workflow: resolve bootstrap and spec chain, verify write authority, make the smallest correct change, run relevant tests and validation, then provide a report of specs used, files changed, verification results, and any remaining uncertainty.
prompt_templates.scenario: Expert: Complete implementation workflow with validation and structured reporting

output_examples.input: Fix the null pointer exception in src/services/auth.js. This is a SpecDD project.
output: Confirmed write authority: spec at .specdd/specs/auth.md section 3 grants write permission
Implementation: Added null check at line 142 of src/services/auth.js
Verification: All existing tests pass, one new test added for null input

output_examples.input: Can I add a new utility function to the shared library? Show me the governance check first.
output: Spec chain for lib/utils: bootstrap.md -> specs/shared.md -> specs/utils.md
Write authority: specs/utils.md section 2.1 - 'Contributors may add new utility functions'
Constraints: specs/shared.md section 5 - 'New utilities must not introduce external dependencies'
Verdict: Change is authorized under current governance, with the constraint that no new external dependencies are added

best_practices: Read bootstrap files only when the active project root changes or exact text is needed for a decision. Do not reread unnecessarily.
Make the smallest correct change inside your write authority. Avoid scope creep or opportunistic unrelated modifications.
Verify write authority from the nearest governing spec before every edit. Never infer authority from similar filenames or conventions.
anti_patterns: Editing files without first resolving the spec chain and confirming write authority from the governing spec.
Rereading the entire spec chain when only the narrowest relevant spec is needed for the current decision.
Completing unrelated tasks opportunistically while implementing a specified change under authority.

faq.question: What is a bootstrap file in SpecDD?
faq.answer: A bootstrap file is a root configuration file that establishes the rules, authority model, and spec resolution process for a SpecDD project. It is always located at .specdd/bootstrap.md.

faq.question: When should I reread bootstrap files during a session?
faq.answer: Reread bootstrap files only when the active project root changes, a bootstrap file may have changed, or you need the exact bootstrap text for a decision, quote, comparison, or report.

faq.question: What does write authority mean in this skill?
faq.answer: Write authority means explicit permission granted by a governing spec to make changes to a specific target. It is identified by reading the nearest local spec and must be confirmed before any edit.

faq.question: Can I modify project specification files?
faq.answer: No. You must not override, loosen, or ignore target project specs. Write authority applies to implementing code changes, not modifying the governance specifications themselves.

faq.question: What happens if the bootstrap.md file is missing?
faq.answer: If .specdd/bootstrap.md is missing, do not continue with this skill. The project is not a SpecDD project. Use specdd-adopt only if the operator explicitly asks to add SpecDD to a new project.

faq.question: How does specdd-do differ from specdd-adopt?
faq.answer: specdd-do is for implementing changes under existing SpecDD authority. specdd-adopt is for setting up SpecDD in a project that does not yet have it. They have separate requirements and should not be used interchangeably.
